mapping.a_element: spatially localized spin droplet nucleated around the defect
mapping.b_element: spatially localized discrete breather on the lattice

mapping.a_element: upper critical dimension of the metallic quantum critical point
mapping.b_element: critical lattice dimension d_c above which breather energy has a positive lower bound

brief_justification: The overlap is only superficial (both feature a spatially localized structure and a dimension threshold), but the driving machinery differs: paper A is a static quantum-critical nucleation phenomenon where an impurity potential coupling to the order-parameter-squared nucleates an order-parameter droplet near a phase transition (with dissipative channels and tunneling suppression), whereas paper B is a classical nonlinear-lattice dynamics problem where self-localized time-periodic solutions arise from nonlinearity/discreteness, with energy thresholds set by amplitude scaling and bifurcation from band-edge plane waves. There is no role-for-role correspondence of governing equations, and the two 'critical dimensions' originate from unrelated mechanisms (RG/critical fluctuations vs. energy-amplitude scaling of a nonlinear mode).
